theme.title: Rotation: Growth → Value & Defensives
theme.summary: Utilities (+2.11%) and Materials (+1.40%) led the market today, while Consumer Discretionary (-0.72%) and Real Estate (-0.66%) brought up the rear. That split lines up with a rotation out of growth: growth-oriented sectors averaged -0.53% versus +0.65% for defensives and +0.42% for value and cyclicals, suggesting money moved from high-multiple names into steadier, cash-generative businesses. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 -0.14%, Dow -0.01%, Nasdaq -0.57% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.92%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 2.40% to 16.64, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.01% to $4,136.10, crude oil was little changed at $87.56, and bitcoin gained 0.20% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
